summary_one_line: Input must begin with the literal SQL keyword 'SELECT ' and a well-formed arithmetic token stream so sqlite3GetToken takes the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes at offsets 0x00-0x06 must spell the SQL token 'SELECT ' (53 45 4c 45 43 54 20) followed by a lexically valid arithmetic-token stream (digits, '+','-','.', '\r') so sqlite3GetToken classifies the next character into the token class whose true branch at line 159609 is exercised.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All 5 cmplog winner seeds start with the exact bytes '53 45 4c 45 43 54 20' (SELECT followed by space) and continue with '8+3' arithmetic plus repeating digit/'+'/'-'/'.' bytes; the byte-diff shows offsets 0x00-0x06 are uniformly S,E,L,E,C,T,space across all 9 winner instances.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds are short malformed strings (e.g. '111E.T', '1ES...', '9999EEEE...') that never reconstruct the 'SELECT ' prefix or a valid arithmetic token sequence, so the tokenizer never reaches the true side at 159609.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog intercepts the integer/string CMP callbacks inside sqlite3GetToken's character-class comparisons and records both operands; I2SRandReplace then substitutes the expected token bytes (SELECT keyword chars and operator chars) into the input, building the valid token stream that naive's blind havoc cannot assemble.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true branch of a comparison inside the SQL tokenizer sqlite3GetToken.
The globally blocked side is the true branch.
Every winner-resolving seed begins with the literal ASCII 'SELECT 8+3' prefix.
Winner inputs are uniformly the SELECT keyword bytes at the first 7 offsets while loser inputs are scattered junk there.
Loser-blocking seeds are short malformed inputs that do not form a valid SELECT token stream.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which reads recorded CMP operands and substitutes the other operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator, only blind havoc/token mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 14791 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 3 --seed f46278e6659d6cfa`; the ancestor chain has 39 levels and the tool reports 'I2S-floor signal: 2 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 37, 38', i.e. two ParentInfo-only (mutation_op = -) ancestors characteristic of unwrapped I2SRandReplace finds in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose true branch is reached at the same rate by naive as by cmplog when the gate requires the literal 'SELECT ' prefix, i.e. removing I2S substitution does not lower the resolution rate.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact comparison at line 159609 is inferred from the tokenizer function name and the SELECT-prefixed winner bytes; the prompt's source overlay shows no annotated source text for the blocker line, so the precise token-class CMP is not directly visible.
